drafts.subject1: Apollo Therapeutics Ltd — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Dave,

I came across your Vaccines, Cardiovascular, Dermatology, Respiratory programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — programmes that incorporate MIDD from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer regulatory surprises.

Our team at PumasAI — pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists, several of whom came from FDA, Pfizer, and BMS — focuses specifically on model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy across all development phases. We've delivered 40+ regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and Health Canada, all accepted on first review. Rather than piecemeal consulting, we handle the entire clinical pharmacology programme end-to-end: model development, analysis, regulatory writing, and agency interactions.



Cardiovascular programmes carry specific regulatory expectations around QTc analysis, exposure-response for both efficacy and cardiac safety, and often thorough organ impairment assessments. These are well within our wheelhouse.Would a short call make sense to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a relevant case study from a similar programme beforehand.
drafts.subject2: Re: Apollo Therapeutics Ltd — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.fu1:
Hi Dave,

Wanted to follow up briefly on my earlier note about your Vaccines, Cardiovascular, Dermatology, Respiratory programme.

Many teams find that proactive MIDD strategy reduces overall development time and strengthens regulatory interactions. Happy to share a relevant case study.

We recently supported a clinical development programme with PK/PD modelling that informed a key regulatory decision, reducing back-and-forth with FDA by several months — happy to share a short write-up if it'd be useful context for your team. No pitch, just the technical detail.

Would it be worth 15 minutes to see if the timing works for Apollo Therapeutics Ltd?
drafts.fu2:
Hi Dave,

I know your inbox is full — this will be my last note on your Vaccines, Cardiovascular, Dermatology, Respiratory programme.

If a pharmacometrics or clinical pharmacology need comes up at any stage, please feel free to reach out.

If the timing isn't right, completely understood. We work with teams at every stage of development, so feel free to reach out whenever it makes sense.

Wishing you and the Apollo Therapeutics Ltd team every success with the programme.

drafts.subject1: GC Biopharma Corp — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Brian,

I came across your Vaccines, Rare & Genetic Diseases programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — programmes that incorporate MIDD from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer regulatory surprises.

Our team at PumasAI — pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists, several of whom came from FDA, Pfizer, and BMS — focuses specifically on model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy across all development phases. We've delivered 40+ regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and Health Canada, all accepted on first review. Rather than piecemeal consulting, we handle the entire clinical pharmacology programme end-to-end: model development, analysis, regulatory writing, and agency interactions.



Rare disease programmes have a unique challenge: small patient populations mean every data point matters. We specialise in model-informed approaches — leveraging Bayesian methods, adaptive designs, and extrapolation strategies — that maximise what you learn from limited data. FDA's Office of Orphan Products has been increasingly receptive to these approaches.Would a short call make sense to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a relevant case study from a similar programme beforehand.

Wanted to follow up briefly on my earlier note about your Dermatology, Infectious Disease, Oncology, Vaccines programme.

Many teams find that proactive MIDD strategy reduces overall development time and strengthens regulatory interactions. Happy to share a relevant case study.

We recently supported a clinical development programme with PK/PD modelling that informed a key regulatory decision, reducing back-and-forth with FDA by several months — happy to share a short write-up if it'd be useful context for your team. No pitch, just the technical detail.

Would it be worth 15 minutes to see if the timing works for Sanaria Inc.?

drafts.subject1: Sanaria Inc. — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Robert,

I came across your Dermatology, Infectious Disease, Oncology, Vaccines programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — programmes that incorporate MIDD from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer regulatory surprises.

Our team at PumasAI — pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists, several of whom came from FDA, Pfizer, and BMS — focuses specifically on model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy across all development phases. We've delivered 40+ regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and Health Canada, all accepted on first review. Rather than piecemeal consulting, we handle the entire clinical pharmacology programme end-to-end: model development, analysis, regulatory writing, and agency interactions.



Oncology programmes face specific regulatory scrutiny around exposure-response for both efficacy and toxicity. A well-characterised E-R model here often makes the difference between a smooth review and a clinical hold or information request.Would a short call make sense to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a relevant case study from a similar programme beforehand.

Wanted to follow up briefly on my earlier note about your Vaccines, Infectious Disease programme.

Many teams find that proactive MIDD strategy reduces overall development time and strengthens regulatory interactions. Happy to share a relevant case study.

We recently supported a clinical development programme with PK/PD modelling that informed a key regulatory decision, reducing back-and-forth with FDA by several months — happy to share a short write-up if it'd be useful context for your team. No pitch, just the technical detail.

Would it be worth 15 minutes to see if the timing works for Vaxcyte?
drafts.fu2:
Hi Gang,

I know your inbox is full — this will be my last note on your Vaccines, Infectious Disease programme.

If a pharmacometrics or clinical pharmacology need comes up at any stage, please feel free to reach out.

If the timing isn't right, completely understood. We work with teams at every stage of development, so feel free to reach out whenever it makes sense.

Wishing you and the Vaxcyte team every success with the programme.
drafts.linkedin: Hi Gang, I noticed Vaxcyte is running a Vaccines, Infectious Disease trial. At PumasAI, we help teams like yours with pharmacometrics, clinical pharmacology, and MIDD strategy. Happy to share a relevant example if useful.

contacts.name: Anjali
contacts.title: EA to the SVP General Counsel and Chief Business & Strategy Officer

drafts.subject1: Vaxcyte — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Anjali,

I came across your Vaccines, Infectious Disease programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — programmes that incorporate MIDD from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer regulatory surprises.

Our team at PumasAI — pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists, several of whom came from FDA, Pfizer, and BMS — focuses specifically on model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy across all development phases. We've delivered 40+ regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and Health Canada, all accepted on first review. Rather than piecemeal consulting, we handle the entire clinical pharmacology programme end-to-end: model development, analysis, regulatory writing, and agency interactions.



Would a short call make sense to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a relevant case study from a similar programme beforehand.

drafts.subject1: Vaxcyte — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Harp,

I came across your Vaccines, Infectious Disease programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — programmes that incorporate MIDD from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer regulatory surprises.

Our team at PumasAI — pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists, several of whom came from FDA, Pfizer, and BMS — focuses specifically on model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy across all development phases. We've delivered 40+ regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and Health Canada, all accepted on first review. Rather than piecemeal consulting, we handle the entire clinical pharmacology programme end-to-end: model development, analysis, regulatory writing, and agency interactions.



Would a short call make sense to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a relevant case study from a similar programme beforehand.
drafts.subject2: Re: Vaxcyte — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.fu1:

Many teams find that proactive MIDD strategy reduces overall development time and strengthens regulatory interactions. Happy to share a relevant case study.

Would it be worth 15 minutes to see if the timing works for Vaxcyte?

drafts.subject1: Summit Therapeutics — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi H,

I came across your Oncology programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — and programmes that build MIDD into their clinical strategy from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer surprises.

At PumasAI, we partner with clinical development teams to deliver model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy. Our scientists are pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists — former Pfizer, FDA, and academia — and we work end-to-end from model development through regulatory submission.

Across your 4-country footprint, regulatory-ready PK/PD packages are a recurring need — something we handle routinely.

Would a 20-minute call be worthwhile to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a brief case study relevant to your programme first.

drafts.subject1: Summit Therapeutics — MIDD & Clinical Pharmacology Support
drafts.email1:
Hi Vivian,

I came across your Oncology programme, and wanted to reach out.

Model-Informed Drug Development is increasingly expected by FDA and EMA reviewers — and programmes that build MIDD into their clinical strategy from the start consistently reach decision points faster with fewer surprises.

At PumasAI, we partner with clinical development teams to deliver model-informed drug development, clinical pharmacology, and regulatory strategy. Our scientists are pharmacometricians and clinical pharmacologists — former Pfizer, FDA, and academia — and we work end-to-end from model development through regulatory submission.

Across your 4-country footprint, regulatory-ready PK/PD packages are a recurring need — something we handle routinely.

Would a 20-minute call be worthwhile to see if there's a fit? Happy to share a brief case study relevant to your programme first.
